title: Historical backfill store + writer (idempotent, resumable)
acceptance_criteria: Migration 007 is idempotent (IF NOT EXISTS throughout) and does NOT alter the existing live-data hypertable PKs.
Adds a `resolution` column to market_ticks and option_ticks (nullable for existing live rows).

Adds a PARTIAL UNIQUE index `(symbol, time) WHERE source='fyers-historical'` (the index MUST include the partition column `time`). Live ingestion writes source='fyers'/'simulator' — a disjoint key space, so the historical index never collides with live writers. Build non-concurrently on the effectively-empty key space; detect and clean up an INVALID index on failure.
Creates table `backfill_ranges(symbol, from_ts, to_ts, resolution, status, rows_written, updated_at)` to track completed/partial ranges (resumable).

Writer consumes T-54's candle output; synthesizes one row per candle (candle close -> ltp, candle time -> time) tagged source='fyers-historical' and the source resolution. Re-running a completed range writes ZERO duplicate rows (ON CONFLICT on the partial-unique key).

Resumable: on T-54's 401 resumable-error, checkpoint progress in backfill_ranges and fail loud with a resume marker; a subsequent run continues from the checkpoint without re-downloading completed data.

Trading-calendar reconciliation: reconcile fetched candle timestamps against the NSE calendar (holidays, expiry-morning half-days) using instrument-registry expiry helpers; mark gaps explicitly in backfill_ranges — NEVER silently mark a gappy range 'complete'.
All hypertable writes/reads are time-range bounded (hypertable discipline).
schema.ts gains typed interfaces for backfill_ranges and the resolution column.
Integration-style test (against the existing test DB harness) proves: idempotent re-run = zero dupes; interrupted run resumes from checkpoint; a calendar gap is marked, not hidden.
Named exports only; TS strict; passes tsc --noEmit and lint.
output_format: code plus plain English explanation of every non-obvious decision
